messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

{"id":"gen-1772255986-8rn6GW93XT1BEmxblpTT","provider":"Moonshot AI","model":"moonshotai/kimi-k2.5","object":"chat.completion","created":1772255986,"choices":[{"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"stop","index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"```json
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\"><rect width=\"512\" height=\"512\" fill=\"#B3E5FC\"/><path d=\"M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 50 170 L 80 256 L 50 342 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256\" fill=\"#455A64\"/><path d=\"M 260 200 L 280 120 L 300 205 Z\" fill=\"#37474F\"/><path d=\"M 300 300 L 340 360 L 280 315 Z\" fill=\"#37474F\"/><circle cx=\"410\" cy=\"240\" r=\"5\" fill=\"#000\"/><circle cx=\"411\" cy=\"239\" r=\"2\" fill=\"#FFF\"/></svg>"
}
```","refusal":null,"reasoning":"The user wants me to draw a "shark" using SVG. The constraints are:
1. JSON output only with a single field "svg"
2. One complete <svg>...</svg> string
3. No text labels, letters, or numbers
4. Simple and valid SVG, no scripts, no foreignObject
5. 512x512 canvas

I need to create a recognizable shark silhouette or illustration. A shark is typically recognized by:
- Streamlined body shape
- Dorsal fin (the iconic triangle on top)
- Tail fin (caudal fin)
- Pectoral fins
- Pointed snout
- Often depicted with an open mouth showing teeth, but that might be too detailed for a simple SVG

I'll create a side view of a shark swimming. I'll use:
- A main body path (elliptical/curved shape)
- Dorsal fin (triangle on top)
- Tail fin (forked or triangular)
- Pectoral fin (bottom side)
- Maybe an eye (simple circle)

Color scheme: Sharks are typically grey/blue on top and white on bottom, but for simplicity I can use a solid grey or gradient.

Let me draft the SVG:
- ViewBox: 0 0 512 512
- Background: maybe light blue for water, or just transparent/white
- Shark positioned in the center, facing right (or left)

Path ideas:
- Body: Start from nose, curve over back to tail, curve under belly back to nose
- Dorsal fin: Triangle around x=250, y=150
- Tail: Two lobes at the back
- Pectoral fin: Triangle on bottom
- Eye: Small circle near the nose

Let me sketch coordinates roughly:
- Nose at (400, 200)
- Tail at (100, 200)
- Top of back at (250, 100)
- Bottom of belly at (250, 280)

Actually, let's make it face left (more traditional). 
- Nose at (420, 256)
- Tail at (80, 256)
- Dorsal fin at (250, 150)

Simplified path for body:
M 420 256 (nose)
Q 380 200 300 180 (upper curve)
Q 200 160 100 200 (toward tail top)
L 80 150 (tail upper)
L 90 256 (tail middle)
L 80 350 (tail lower)
Q 200 350 300 330 (lower curve back to nose)
Q 380 310 420 256 (close to nose)

Dorsal fin:
M 220 180
L 250 80
L 280 185
Z

Pectoral fin:
M 280 300
L 320 380
L 240 320
Z

Eye:
Circle at (390, 240) radius 5, fill black or dark grey

Gills: Three curved lines at (340, 256), (350, 256), (360, 256)

Let me refine this into proper SVG syntax.

Color: #607D8B (blue-grey) for body, #455A64 for darker parts, white for belly if I want to add detail, but simple is better.

Actually, looking at the constraints, I should keep it very clean and recognizable. Let me make a stylized shark:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <!-- Water background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#E3F2FD"/>
  
  <!-- Shark body -->
  <path d="M 420 256 Q 350 180 250 200 Q 150 220 100 200 L 80 160 L 90 256 L 80 352 L 100 312 Q 150 292 250 312 Q 350 332 420 256 Z" fill="#546E7A"/>
  
  <!-- Dorsal fin -->
  <path d="M 240 205 L 260 120 L 290 210 Z" fill="#455A64"/>
  
  <!-- Pectoral fin -->
  <path d="M 260 300 L 300 360 L 240 320 Z" fill="#455A64"/>
  
  <!-- Eye -->
  <circle cx="390" cy="240" r="8" fill="#000"/>
  <circle cx="392" cy="238" r="3" fill="#FFF"/>
  
  <!-- Gills -->
  <path d="M 340 240 Q 330 256 340 272" stroke="#37474F" stroke-width="3" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M 350 238 Q 340 256 350 274" stroke="#37474F" stroke-width="3" fill="none"/>
  <path d="M 360 236 Q 350 256 360 276" stroke="#37474F" stroke-width="3" fill="none"/>
</svg>

Wait, I should check if this looks like a shark. The body shape needs to be more elongated and streamlined. Let me adjust:

Better approach:
- Main body: elongated ellipse-like shape
- Tail: heterocercal (upper lobe longer than lower)
- Dorsal fin: triangular, centered

Revised coordinates:
- Nose: (450, 256)
- Tail start: (100, 256)
- Tail upper: (60, 180)
- Tail lower: (60, 332)
- Belly curve: (250, 320)
- Back curve: (250, 192)

Path: M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 60 180 L 80 256 L 60 332 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256

Dorsal: M 220 205 L 240 140 L 270 210 Z

Pectoral: M 280 300 L 320 350 L 260 315 Z

Eye: (420, 246) r=6

This should look better. I'll use a nice blue-grey color scheme.

Also, I should ensure the SVG is valid and complete. I'll add xmlns attribute and viewBox.

Final check: No text, no numbers as text elements (coordinates in paths are fine), 512x512 canvas.

One more check - the user wants JSON output. So:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">...</svg>"
}

I need to escape quotes properly in the JSON string.

Let me construct the final SVG string:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#81D4FA"/>
  <path d="M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 60 180 L 80 256 L 60 332 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256" fill="#455A64"/>
  <path d="M 220 205 L 240 140 L 270 210 Z" fill="#37474F"/>
  <path d="M 280 300 L 320 350 L 260 315 Z" fill="#37474F"/>
  <circle cx="420" cy="246" r="5" fill="#000"/>
  <circle cx="421" cy="245" r="2" fill="#FFF"/>
</svg>

This looks like a shark swimming to the right. The background is light blue (water), the shark is dark grey/blue-grey. It has the distinctive dorsal fin and tail shape.

Actually, I should make sure the tail looks more like a shark tail (heterocercal). Currently both lobes are somewhat equal. Let me adjust:
- Upper tail lobe: (60, 180) 
- Lower tail lobe: (80, 320) - wait, that's not right.

Shark tails: The vertebral column extends into the upper lobe, making it longer. So:
- Upper lobe tip: (50, 170)
- Lower lobe tip: (80, 340)
- Connection to body: (100, 256)

So:
M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 50 170 L 80 256 L 50 342 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256

Yes, that gives the upper lobe longer and angled up, lower lobe shorter and angled down - classic shark tail.

Let me verify the pectoral fin position. It should be behind the gills, on the side/bottom. My coordinates (280, 300) seem reasonable for a side view.

The dorsal fin at (240, 140) with base from (220, 205) to (270, 210) - that's a bit wide. Let me adjust:
M 230 200 L 250 130 L 270 205 Z

Actually, looking at shark anatomy, the dorsal fin is usually about midway on the back. Let me place it around x=280 (between nose at 450 and tail at 100, so around 275 is middle).

Refined:
Body path: M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 50 170 L 80 256 L 50 342 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256
Dorsal: M 260 200 L 280 120 L 300 205 Z
Pectoral: M 300 300 L 340 360 L 280 315 Z
Eye: cx="410" cy="240" r="5"

</svg>

Wait, I should check if this looks like a shark. The body shape needs to be more elongated and streamlined. Let me adjust:

Better approach:
- Main body: elongated ellipse-like shape
- Tail: heterocercal (upper lobe longer than lower)
- Dorsal fin: triangular, centered

Revised coordinates:
- Nose: (450, 256)
- Tail start: (100, 256)
- Tail upper: (60, 180)
- Tail lower: (60, 332)
- Belly curve: (250, 320)
- Back curve: (250, 192)

Pectoral: M 280 300 L 320 350 L 260 315 Z

Eye: (420, 246) r=6

This should look better. I'll use a nice blue-grey color scheme.

Also, I should ensure the SVG is valid and complete. I'll add xmlns attribute and viewBox.

Final check: No text, no numbers as text elements (coordinates in paths are fine), 512x512 canvas.

One more check - the user wants JSON output. So:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns=\"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg\" viewBox=\"0 0 512 512\">...</svg>"
}

I need to escape quotes properly in the JSON string.

Let me construct the final SVG string:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 512 512">

</svg>

This looks like a shark swimming to the right. The background is light blue (water), the shark is dark grey/blue-grey. It has the distinctive dorsal fin and tail shape.

Actually, I should make sure the tail looks more like a shark tail (heterocercal). Currently both lobes are somewhat equal. Let me adjust:
- Upper tail lobe: (60, 180) 
- Lower tail lobe: (80, 320) - wait, that's not right.

Shark tails: The vertebral column extends into the upper lobe, making it longer. So:
- Upper lobe tip: (50, 170)
- Lower lobe tip: (80, 340)
- Connection to body: (100, 256)

So:
M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 50 170 L 80 256 L 50 342 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256

Yes, that gives the upper lobe longer and angled up, lower lobe shorter and angled down - classic shark tail.

Let me verify the pectoral fin position. It should be behind the gills, on the side/bottom. My coordinates (280, 300) seem reasonable for a side view.

The dorsal fin at (240, 140) with base from (220, 205) to (270, 210) - that's a bit wide. Let me adjust:
M 230 200 L 250 130 L 270 205 Z

Actually, looking at shark anatomy, the dorsal fin is usually about midway on the back. Let me place it around x=280 (between nose at 450 and tail at 100, so around 275 is middle).

Refined:
Body path: M 450 256 Q 380 190 280 200 Q 180 210 100 200 L 50 170 L 80 256 L 50 342 L 100 312 Q 180 302 280 312 Q 380 322 450 256
Dorsal: M 260 200 L 280 120 L 300 205 Z
Pectoral: M 300 300 L 340 360 L 280 315 Z
Eye: cx="410" cy="240" r="5"

This should be good. I'll make the JSON now.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
